Depends on what kind of IC design you want to pursue.
If it is analog, then you need to know both digital and some device knowledge
If it is digital, then you need to know some device knowledge but you may not need to know analog circuit.
If it is RF, then you need both digital, analog, some device knowledge and some EM theory.

You can get a digital design position with a bachelor's degree. For analog, it will usually require a Master's degree. RF is usually for phd. One exception is you have worked as an intern, and they think you can design without an advanced degree.

Depends on what kind of IC design you want to pursue.
If it is analog, then you need to know both digital and some device knowledge
If it is digital, then you need to know some device knowledge but you may not need to know analog circuit.
If it is RF, then you need both digital, analog, some device knowledge and some EM theory.

You can get a digital design position with a bachelor's degree. For analog, it will usually require a Master's degree. RF is usually for phd. One exception is you have worked as an intern, and they think you can design without an advanced degree.

Depends on what kind of IC design you want to pursue.
If it is analog, then you need to know both digital and some device knowledge
If it is digital, then you need to know some device knowledge but you may not need to know analog circuit.
If it is RF, then you need both digital, analog, some device knowledge and some EM theory.
